name: Reconcile DNS desired → observed through authoritative provider
description: Boundary-class provider-aware process. The Plesk connector is the unified URI facade, while its receipt truthfully identifies Cloudflare or Plesk as the authoritative DNS provider. Apply requires human approval, an exact plan_hash and a provider credential leased from vault.

uri_processes.name: Load desired DNS from repo SSOT

payload.note: Stub: wire to real fs:// or inline payload when registering pack

uri_processes.name: Prepare or apply DNS reconcile (HITL — provider write)

notes: Connector routes exist in urirun-connector-plesk v0.12.0; urirun-node must refresh its connector image/registry before execution.
Dry-run checks authority and the provider API state; it never changes DNS.
Apply mutate step stays human_approval=true — never zero-touch production DNS.
2026-07-21 public observe: docs.subactor.com and docs-stage.subactor.com resolve to A 217.160.250.222 through Cloudflare authoritative nameservers.
Until the cloudflare-dns vault entry exists, authority and propagation queries work but provider API dry-run/apply fails closed.
Cloudflare batch changes are transactional in its database, but distributed DNS propagation remains observable as a separate step.
